title: AI-Driven Vulnerability Prioritization in Practice
modules: Why CVSS Scores Alone Are Failing Security Teams
How AI Models Estimate Exploitation Probability from Real-World Data
Feeding AI Prioritization Tools: Data Quality, Bias, and Coverage
Hands-On: Comparing AI-Assisted vs. Manual Triage Workflows
Integrating AI Prioritization Into Existing Patch Management Pipelines
Measuring and Validating Prioritization Accuracy Over Time
Governance and Accountability When AI Drives Remediation Decisions
synopsis: Security teams drown in vulnerability alerts, and traditional severity scoring leaves the most exploitable flaws buried under noise — a gap that attackers have learned to exploit. This course shows how AI-powered prioritization models use threat intelligence, exploit availability, and environmental context to surface the vulnerabilities that actually get weaponized, and how to integrate those signals into real workflows.
rationale: AI-assisted vulnerability prioritization is rapidly becoming a foundational security operations capability, yet practitioners lack structured guidance on how these models work, where they fail, and how to operationalize them responsibly.
learning_outcome: Learners can evaluate, configure, and critically interrogate an AI-assisted vulnerability prioritization tool and integrate its outputs into a defensible, auditable patch management workflow.
source_signals: Vulnerability Exploitation Probability Metric Proposed by NIST, CISA Researchers
Small models also found the vulnerabilities that Mythos found
nearest_existing: Every generation of programmers has faced a tool that rewrites what review even means
